entry.title: Re: Become an Early Adopter for Joule in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@Tommaso 

you can find the information here: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-catalog/?searchValue=datasphere 

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Challenge in Assigning admin role to business user in...
entry.content:
Hello,

Steps to Assign Admin Role:

  1. Access SAP Fiori Launchpad:

    • Ensure you are logged into the SAP Fiori Launchpad using appropriate credentials.

  2. Navigate to ‘Maintain Business Roles’:

    • Open the Maintain Business Roles app. Creating a Business Role and Assigning Business Users (Administrator)This app allows you to create or modify business roles.

  3. Create or Select Business Role:

    • If you haven’t created a business role for administrators yet, use the option â€˜Create from Templateâ€™ to select a suitable template.
    • Choose, for instance, SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR as the template.

  4. Assign Users to Role:

    • Once the business role is created, navigate to the ‘Assigned Business Users’ tab.
    • Use the Add button to search and assign the business user you want to provide admin access to.

  5. Review Permission Settings:

    • Ensure that the permissions for Read, Write, and Value Help are set to Unrestricted, especially if creating a new role.

  6. Save Changes:

    • Once changes are made, save them to ensure the business user has been assigned the correct role.

Troubleshooting Tips:

  • Ensure Proper System Access: Verify that you have access to the test environment as required by your learning journey.
  • Check Authorization: Sometimes, issues might occur if the account you’re using doesn’t have the right permissions to make changes.
  • Re-login: Occasionally, a simple re-login to the SAP Fiori Launchpad can resolve issues if the interface isn’t responding.

If you cannot find the Maintain Business Users App, you may not have the proper roles. If you click on the app but the app does not open, it could be your system has errors. You may try in a different system.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How to Show All Receipts Across Multiple POS Systems...
entry.content:
Hello

I just like to add a possible solution for this. You could add function 4102 "Display Customer's Last Purchased Receipts" to your Quick Selection. So based on a specific customer, this could be a possible solution.

Best Regards
Klaus

entry.title: Re: How to identify vulnerabilities according to our SAP...
entry.content: https://support.sap.com/en/my-support/knowledge-base/security-notes-news.html

entry.title: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors HCM: Highlights from SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
Top Takeaways for HR Leaders from SAP Sapphire 2025

SAP Sapphire 2025 delivered a bold message, the future of Human Capital Management (HCM) is intelligent, agile, and AI-powered. With new product launches, strategic pivots, and major updates across analytics, service delivery, and digital adoption, SAP is leading the next evolution of HR transformation.

These innovations draw on SAP’s unparalleled integration of business data and enterprise-grade AI - helping HR leaders make smarter decisions, deliver better experiences, and connect people strategy to business success like never before.

Discover what’s next in human capital management and what it means for your team.

Key Innovations in SAP SuccessFactors HCM

1. People Intelligence in SAP Business Data Cloud

Announced during Dan Beck’s HCM keynote, People Intelligence is SAP's next-generation people analytics platform, now integrated with the SAP Business Data Cloud. This solution unifies people and skills data from SAP SuccessFactors with broader business datasets, turning it into actionable, AI-powered insights for HR and business leaders.

Highlights:

  • Self-service, business-friendly interface

  • Hundreds of HR KPIs and AI-based forecasts

  • Governed, enriched workforce data for decision-making

  • Joule copilot integration for instant answers

People Intelligence shall go General Availability sometime in Q3 2025.
Target audience: EC + Workforce Analytics customers aligning with SAP's analytics stack.

2. Performance and Goals Agent

The new Performance and Goals Agent is designed to supercharge the employee-manager relationship. The Performance and Goals Agent aims to enhance performance management by equipping managers with the information they need to have productive and insightful conversations with their team members. 

 
Functionality:
The agent utilizes data-driven insights to help managers identify areas for improvement, highlight employee strengths, and tailor development plans.

Availability:
While the agent was announced at SAP Sapphire 2025, its general availability is planned for Q4 2025. 

3. WalkMe for SAP SuccessFactors HCM

In collaboration with WalkMe, SAP SuccessFactors now features AI-powered in-app guidance across key workflows like onboarding and time-off requests.

Key capabilities:

  • Click-by-click user journey tracking

  • AI-powered step-by-step support and automation

  • Built-in guides for onboarding, compliance, and training

  • Customizable packages for enterprise-wide digital adoption

Availability: Embedded by default effective 1H 2025, with upgrade options to Platform and Premium WalkMe licenses.

4. SAP SuccessFactors Enterprise Service Management

Replacing the legacy ECSC (Employee Central Service Center), this new solution delivers purpose-built, AI-powered HR case management and self-service. This new solution brings AI-based case management and self-service HR capabilities into one unified experience.

Features:

  • Intelligent case routing and prioritization with Joule AI

  • Customized case flows for complex HR scenarios

  • Proactive support and sentiment analysis

  • Seamless integration with Employee Central master data

Availability: SKU is now active. ECSC customers will need reimplementation; migration tools and support are in development.

5. New SAP Business Suite Packages for HCM

To further accelerate transformation, SAP is introducing modular SAP Business Suite Packages for HCM. These are pre-integrated bundles of HR capabilities built on SAP BTP that simplify implementation and reduce time-to-value.

These packages provide integrated solutions across:

  • Core HR

  • Workforce planning and analytics

  • HR service delivery

  • AI and automation

Packages allow HR teams to:

  • Choose capabilities based on current needs
  • Scale and integrate easily over time
  • Leverage the full power of SAP Business AI and analytics

Ideal for CHROs looking for a fast, scalable route to value with deep integration and extensibility.

Key Takeaways for HR Leaders

  • AI that Drives Impact: SAP’s embedded AI enables HR teams to make decisions faster and with confidence.
  • Smarter, More Engaged Employees: Tools like WalkMe and the Performance Agent create better experiences and improve adoption.
  • Data-Driven Workforce Strategy: People Intelligence empowers leaders to align skills, roles, and resources with business strategy.
  • Faster, Simpler Transformation: SAP’s modular HCM packages reduce complexity and help teams move quickly from planning to value realization.

Final Thoughts

Your best. Made real. Powered by SAP SuccessFactors.

In an era marked by constant change and workforce uncertainty, SAP’s latest HCM innovations offer a roadmap for resilient, data-driven HR transformation.

By combining AI, people insights, and enterprise integration, SAP SuccessFactors continues to set the benchmark for modern HCM — helping organizations around the world not only keep up, but lead the way forward.

For a complete view of all innovations announced at SAP Sapphire 2025, visit the:
SAP Sapphire 2025 Innovation Guide

SAP Sapphire 2025 continues in:

  • Madrid: May 26–28

entry.title: SAP ME- meint queue error -"Operation XXX does not exists "
entry.content:
Hello all, Good Day!

We are facing issue with one production order in SAP ME.

Shop order idoc failing with meint queue error message -"Operation XXX does not exists ".We checked for operations availability in operation maintenance-> operation is present with "obsolete" status, we changed it to "releasable" and retried failed IDOC, but error still there.

Kindly help with solution.

Thanks in advance.

Jyoti.

entry.title: Re: WalkMe Now Available in SAP SuccessFactors : A New Era of Digital Adoption
entry.content:
Hi there.

I have a client that has tried it in their build environment and noticed it only seems to work for users with the English (US) language. They wanted to check if there’s anything they can do to make it available for users with other languages too?

Is this only available in the 1H release in English (US) only, or does the user see it in their own locale when accessing SuccessFactors?

They are mainly thinking of using it for the Spotlight feature.

Thanks,
Paul Fenwick

entry.title: Re: stock transfer error
entry.content:
Create the material in the mentioned plant.

  • Using either MM02 or the Fiori app F1602.

entry.title: Looking for Insights: PIP (Performance Improvement Process) Implementation in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We’re currently in the early stages of implementing a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) process in SuccessFactors, and I’d really appreciate learning from others who may already be using this functionality.

We’ve started with a basic template in the Performance module, but we’re particularly interested in understanding how other organizations handle the iterative discussions between managers and employees that happen during the PIP process—especially prior to the final review.

If your organization is using PIPs in SuccessFactors, I’d be grateful for your input on:

  • How these discussions are documented (which module or feature do you use?)

  • How edit permissions are managed, so that finalized notes remain locked, but future discussions can still be added and updated

  • Any advice on workflow setup, visibility, and progress tracking

Thanks in advance for sharing your experience!

entry.title: Re: (APIs) TransportationCode -1 not acquired
entry.content:
Hi dausto,

If you would like to keep the same value on Lines, you will have to update the document twice.

In the first update, set value on the header as Null and change the value on lines to some other value as below:

PATCH /b1s/v1/Orders(1234)
{
    "TransportationCode": null,
    "DocumentLines": [
        {
            "LineNum": 0,
            "ShippingMethod": 2
        }
    ]
}

In the second update, set the desired value on the lines.

PATCH /b1s/v1/Orders(1234)
{
    "TransportationCode": null,
    "DocumentLines": [
        {
            "LineNum": 0,
            "ShippingMethod": 3
        }
    ]
}

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

thank you for this extensive blog with useful insights!

I have a question to the sales price (maybe the same question was raised above already). I need to influence the price determination by using either "overtime category" or "work location" from the timsheet record posted by the employee.

These fields do influence the internal cost rate, but I have not yet found a way to influence the sales price based on these fields.

Do you see any option for doing this? 

Thanks, Johannes

entry.title: Resolving Carrier Mismatch Errors When planning Freight Units within SAP Transportation Management
entry.content:
Transportation is a complex process, often requiring careful coordination of Transportation planners and various supply chain partners. This may require the Transportation planners to replan / reassign the demand (Freight Units) to the capacity (Freight order / Trucks). At times, one issue that can arise is a carrier mismatch error when trying to reassign Freight Units (FUs) to Freight Orders (FOs) after they have been unplanned. In this blog, we will explore this issue and offer some possible options to avoid this issue.

Let’s first look at the details of this issue.

When Freight Units are unplanned from an existing Freight Order, they become visible as unplanned in the SAP Transportation Management cockpit. However, attempting to reschedule them into a different freight order can result (under certain conditions) in a carrier mismatch error. This issue arises when the carrier for the freight units is "derived from predecessor documents," creating a discrepancy if the new Freight Order has a different carrier.

Here is a typical process that leads to this error:

1. Create a Sales order and a Freight Unit (FU) and, using the transportation cockpit, create a Road Freight Order (FO) based on the FU.
2. Open the FO and assign a carrier to it. Save your changes.
3. Delete the FO using the appropriate option within the Transportation Cockpit.
4. Next, create a new, empty FO with a different carrier and attempt to assign the initial FU to the newly created FO by selecting both and clicking "Assign Selected Items.”
5. Save the assignment process and encounter no error at this point.

However, the process can also take place when you have a Delivery created. Here the Freight unit is planned on the FO and the carrier may be assigned either to the delivery or to the Freight Order. Now, when the associated FU is unplanned from the FO, the carrier information remains attached to both the FU and the delivery. As you can see in the below screenshot it mentions “Derived from Predecessor Documents”.

At this point if an assignment is planned to a new FO with a different carrier it leads to the system giving an error “Freight unit xxxx cannot be assigned to Freight Order yyyy, carrier doesn't match”.

The system's retention of the carrier information can be due to various scenarios, such as the carrier being inherent in the delivery and thus needing to be retained despite the unplanning of Freight Order and Freight Unit. There can also be a scenario where the transportation planner may only be changing the Freight Order / Truck and continuing with the existing carrier.

To overcome this challenge, one might consider the following strategies

  1. When Carrier was assigned within Freight order
    1. Remove the Carrier from FO1 Before Unplanning / Deletion: By clearing the carrier assignment before unplanning / deleting the FO, you ensure that the FU / delivery has no carrier association and can be reassigned freely.
    2. Assign Carrier After Planning: Once you have unplanned the FU and need to reassign it, place it on a FO without a carrier and then assign the carrier to FO post-planning.
  2. When Carrier was assigned within Delivery
    1.  Delete Carrier from Delivery: Ensure that after FO Unplanning / deletion, the FU and delivery have no carrier specified. This allows reassignment to another FO without issues.

While carrier mismatch is a standard behavior that can occur due to multiple scenarios (some of which are mentioned above), understanding the underlying system logic and utilizing these strategies can facilitate smoother freight unit planning and assignment processes. Kindly note that there can be other options also that can be explored.

Should these solutions not address all nuances of your case, consider submitting a feature request on the [SAP Customer Influence Portal] (https://me.sap.com/notes/0002963059).

entry.title: Re: Moving Q-stock between Plants
entry.content: Hi Janbuchmann. Thanks, I've tried. This is valid idea, but here we are missing "shipping process which is important"

entry.title: Re: How to add custom menu button in the object page head...
entry.content: Example should looks like that

entry.title: Re: How to add custom menu button in the object page head...
entry.content: Example should looks like that "actions": { "showJobManagerAction": { "text": "Rework Required", "press": "com.sap.apm.rcmds.reusable.ext.CustomActions.onReworkRequired" }, "changeToInProcessAction": { "press": "com.sap.apm.rcmds.reusable.ext.CustomActions.onChangeToInProcess", "text": "{status_code}", "enabled": "{status_code ===}" }, "changeToObsoleteAction": { "press": "com.sap.apm.rcmds.reusable.ext.CustomActions.changeStatus", "text": "Status 2", "enabled": "{= ${ui>/isEditable}}" }, "changeToReopenedAction": { "press": "com.sap.apm.rcmds.reusable.ext.CustomActions.changeStatus", "text": "Status 3", "enabled": "{= !${ui>/isEditable}}" }, "MenuActions": { "text": "Change Status", "visible": "true", "menu": [ "changeToInProcessAction", "changeToObsoleteAction", "changeToReopenedAction" ] } },

entry.title: SAP Market rates management - multiple sources
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we are planning to implement the SAP Market Rates Management – Bring Your Own Rates (BYOR) solution to enable the automatic upload and integration of market rates into our client's SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public system. Before we implement the solution, our interest lies in understanding whether the product's functionalities will support uploading currency exchange type M from 3 different sources based on the currency pair?

E.G. if the pair will be:

- Any currency to EUR - source will be ECB

- Any currency to RON - source will be National bank of Romania

- Any currency to CZK - source will be Czech national bank

Thank you in advance,
JB

entry.title: Change Process in CPQ – BRIM S/4 Solution Quotation Integration
entry.content:
Change is the only constant thing is a common knowledge, so how can business documents be an exception to this? It is not! We live in constantly evolving dynamic times, where business strategies, negotiations and deals are also being adapted and changed based on multitude of influencing factors. So, with every change, how the change is managed and how we execute and plan for change especially when it comes to contractual documents like Subscription contract is the topic for this blog.

The CPQ – BRIM integration enables SAP customers to have the best of on cloud CPQ for quoting process and on-prem BRIM for follow-on contract document creation to further proceed with the quote to cash scenario for revenue generation.  As part of contract lifecycle management, for subscription products, this process further extends to support changes to existing subscription contracts. These changes can be made to existing contracts or new contracts can be created for the changes triggered from an existing contract.

Changes like contract extension, renewal, cancellation, change in quantity/ price/configuration adding new products, swapping existing product with new products etc. are possible. Only specific change processes are available based on the state of the contract, e.g. cancelled contract cannot be extended or renewed.

Apart from BRIM prerequisites and configurations,  administrators need to enable access rights for the Assets Table Management and Change Processes sections as explained in Assign Access Rights for Setup Sections and Entities , else the sections won’t be visible in the Setup.

How subscription contract change-process works?

The term Change Process here refers to a singular change that can be executed on a subscription contract document. These Change Processes when group together based on business need and flexibility required form a group of processes called Change Process Groups. A single Change Process can belong to multiple change process groups. Example we have change process groups like Change Quantity and Price, where Change Quantity and Change Price are grouped together. Change Process Group column is synced from S/4 HANA, it can be renamed as per required. This column is reflected in the Assets section of the quote page. Kindly note not all change process in S/4 HANA are supported in this integration. Depending on the change process/group selected in the Asset Section, the subsequent fields in the product section become editable. The editability of the fields can be controlled from the Set-up section. From the above example, the quantity and price can be set to editable.

 

Special change process features like New Contract, Co-Term and Swap, must be additionally set for specific change process groups to further control the and align the behaviour to organization needs. New contract enables the sales rep to either create a new contract from the existing contracts or merge all contracts into one. On placing the order, the new contract is created in SAP S/4 HANA. The old contracts are cancelled, and the references are available in SAP S/4 HANA subscription contract document.

 

To change a particular Subscription contract or to be more specific the items of a subscription contract, we must add it to the quote via the Add Assets “+” icon. These items are termed as Assets and added to the Asset section of the quote. This Asset section can also be personalised via the set-up. The subscription contracts tied to the selected Involved Party in Quotes page are available to be added to quote as Assets.

 

 

 

After adding Assets to the quote page, if the toggle in the quote header section is turned ON to create a new subscription contract, the subsequent changes to the assets selected from one or more contracts will be transferred to a new contract starting on the day of initiating the changes and the existing contracts will be discontinued.

Mostly every change process has direct impact price or value of the quote. Contract Change Activation Date is the reference taken for adjusting all quote relevant price calculations so that prices are always in line with the changes and applicable from the date changes are implemented.

To conclude, for subscription products the SAP CPQ – BRIM integration offers great flexibility to change the already created subscription contracts with feasibility to group multiple changes, change multiple contracts at the same time create new contracts, merge contracts, cancel contracts etc. to enabling smooth management of the contract lifecycle.

Summary Settings for change process

SAP CPQ Set-UpSAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration/iflows)Solution Quotation in S/4 HANA BRIM
Quote Layout : Add Asset SectionRead Change Process Groups for Quote from SAP BRIMSubscription Contracts
Quotes -Assets Table Management : Configure the view of Asset TableRead Change Process for Quote from SAP BRIMChange Processes
Workflow: Preparing – Preparing: Action – Add Asset to Quote and other actionsRead allowed Change Process Groups for Subscription Contract Item from SAP BRIMChange Process Group
Quotes- Change Processes – Add Change Process, group and give relevant names to the group. Additional settings can be maintainedRead Subscription contract header from BRIM 
 Read Subscription contract items from BRIM 
 Replicate Change Contract Processing Status from SAP S4HANA 

 

Most used Change Process Capabilities

Sno

 Change Processes/ Capabilities

Description

Additional Info

1

New Contract

Enables to renew or change the existing subscription contract and create a new contract with reference to it.

On placing the order, the new/merged contract is created in SAP S/4 HANA. The old contracts are cancelled, and the references are available in SAP S/4 HANA subscription contract document.

2

Merge

Enables consolidation of multiple contracts into one contract.

3

Product Change/Swap

Enables the change/replacement of an existing subscription product in a contract.

In Set up -Quote -Change Processes -Change Process Group Tab, for the selected change process group, you must select the values for additional settings to Swap.

4

Co-Term

Enables user to co-term – adjust to the same contract duration for different items in a contact.

 

5

Cancellation

Enables users to cancel a contract item.

Cancellation process needs to be set-up.

6

Contract Extension

Enables the extension of the contact term as set-up in product master data).

 

7

Change Item Quantity

Enables users to change the item quantity for products in a contract.

 

8

Change Configuration 

Enables users to modify the variant configuration-based characteristics for the configurable products in a contract.

 

9

Change Price

Enables users to change the price of the products in the contract

 

10

Add Sub-item

Enables the addition of sub-items during change processes

 

 

Disclaimer: Given the dynamic nature of technology and features, and the potential limits to the author's knowledge, please consider the contents of this blog as an attempt to simplify the subject matter and disseminate further information about it. Therefore, it is recommended to verify the details from official sources or conduct further research to ensure complete accuracy and up-to-date information. The insights provided are meant to foster learning and discussion, not to serve as definitive guidance.

entry.title: Re: 关于月度结算请求处理
entry.content:
Machine translation:

About Monthly Closing Request Processing

You want to automate the monthly closing of projects only for the current month by creating a monthly closing request processing job. However, it was found that when performing settlement processing activities (use APP: 締EITHER Please take care of this concept in Japan), the system will not only process the items for the current month but also book the next month's closing process if there is a sales document that closes the project next month. Details are as follows (issue with May and June closing): +Billing date May 12 → May closing (correct) 2 Billing date May 15 → May settlement (correct) API_Billing date May 21 → June settlement (Issue: Should have been processed in June, but it was processed in May) In this case, settlement processing in May and June will be registered. But I want the documents to be closed in June and should be registered for settlement processing in the job executed on June 21. If there is something unclear about the description, please follow up. How should I set it up please thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: WalkMe Now Available in SAP SuccessFactors : A New Era of Digital Adoption
entry.content:
I've looked at the What's New Viewer for the update on the newly released DAP options with the Guided Tours that have been created and are managed by SAP SuccessFactors.

See here for full details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-release-information/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/018a8cb74e244cf1b848d96746c311b2.html?parentHref=/whats-new/8fcf4960eea24f78b1d7613da406a885?Version=1H+2025&q=tour&locale=en-US&parentName=SAP%20SuccessFactors%20What's%20New&locale=en-US

To access the SAP SuccessFactors-managed content, you need to enable WalkMe in your instances, as it is disabled by default.

After you enable WalkMe, the pre-built guided tours are available to users based on two conditions: the feature must be enabled and configured and the user must have the necessary permissions. If both conditions are met, users can access the guided tour for that feature.

However two very important points to note from the release documention.

  1. Currently, our guided tours are only available when the system language is set to English (US)
  2. You can't enable both SAP SuccessFactors-Managed content and Customer-Managed content at the same time.


The first point confirms your point that the SF created content currently does not support multiple languages. Then on the second point, it also suggests that even if you had the WalkMe DAP product licensed to use, you cannot seem to be able to "mix" content.

entry.title: Fusion Forward: Unlocking Innovation with SAP Build – Series 1: What is Fusion Development?
entry.content:
In this post, you’ll learn how fusion development combines the strengths of citizen and professional developers using SAP Build and SAP Business Technology Platform to create fast, cost-effective, and business-aligned applications—covering the collaborative process, real-world examples, and how to get started with this innovative approach.

What is Fusion Development?

Fusion development is a game-changing approach that blends the expertise of professional developers (who code complex logic and ensure system scalability) with citizen developers (business users who understand processes but don’t code). By combining these strengths, fusion development creates applications that are both technically robust and perfectly aligned with business goals.

At its core, fusion development is about collaboration. It bridges the gap between IT and business teams, enabling them to work together seamlessly. With SAP Build, a low-code platform, citizen developers use drag-and-drop tools to design user interfaces, while professional developers handle integrations and backend logic using SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). The result? Custom applications delivered faster, at lower cost, and with greater relevance to business needs.

 

Real-World Analogy: Think of fusion development as a culinary collaboration. Citizen developers choose the ingredients—defining what the dish (app) should look like and do—while professional developers cook it, ensuring it’s perfectly seasoned and structurally sound. Together, they create a masterpiece that satisfies everyone.

Why Fusion Development Matters

In today’s fast-paced world, businesses need agile solutions to stay competitive. Fusion development delivers by:

  • Empowering Everyone: Non-coders, like store managers or finance teams, can create apps, reducing reliance on IT.
  • Accelerating Delivery: Apps that once took months can now be built in weeks, thanks to low-code tools.
  • Enhancing Collaboration: IT and business teams work hand-in-hand, ensuring solutions meet both technical and practical needs.
  • Maximizing Investments: For SAP customers, fusion development extends existing systems like SAP S/4HANA without costly customizations.

Example in Action: Imagine a retail chain needing an app to track inventory during a holiday rush. A store manager uses SAP Build Apps to design a simple barcode-scanning interface, while an IT developer integrates it with SAP S/4HANA for real-time updates. In just weeks, the app is live, streamlining operations and boosting efficiency.

The Power of SAP Build

SAP Build is the engine behind fusion development, offering a suite of tools designed for collaboration:

  • SAP Build Apps: Enables citizen developers to create intuitive mobile or web interfaces without coding.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: Automates workflows, like approvals or notifications, to streamline processes.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Provides professional developers with tools for backend development, integrations, and scalability.

These tools work together to create a seamless development experience. For example, a citizen developer might build a customer feedback app, while an IT developer connects it to SAP systems for data analysis—all within the same platform.

Benefits of Fusion Development

Fusion development offers transformative advantages:

  • Speed: A logistics firm built a delivery tracking app in two weeks, compared to months with traditional methods.
  • Accessibility: Retail managers create inventory apps, freeing IT for strategic projects.
  • Innovation: A startup prototyped a customer feedback app in days, adapting quickly to market needs.
  • Cost Efficiency: Pre-built templates in SAP Build cut development costs significantly.

What’s Next in the Series?

This is just the beginning! In the next posts, we’ll explore:

  • Real-world case studies, like automating invoice processing.
  • Step-by-step guides to building your own fusion development apps.

Get Started with Fusion Development

Fusion development is more than a trend—it’s a new way to innovate. With SAP Build, anyone can contribute to building powerful, business-driven applications. Ready to dive in? Join the SAP Builders community, explore SAP Build tutorials, or share your thoughts in the comments below. Let’s unlock innovation together!

Call to Action: Stay tuned for Series 2, where we’ll dive into a real-world example of fusion development in action. follow for updates!

Conclusion:
Fusion development is revolutionizing how businesses create applications by uniting the creativity of citizen developers with the expertise of professional developers. Powered by tools like SAP Build and SAP Business Technology Platform, this collaborative approach delivers faster, cost-effective, and business-aligned solutions. From empowering non-coders to accelerating innovation, fusion development enables organizations to stay agile and competitive in a rapidly evolving world. As we continue this series, get ready to explore real-world success stories and practical steps to harness the power of fusion development. Join the SAP Builders community today and start building the future of business applications!

Please feel free to share your thoughts in the comments section below.

If you enjoyed reading this blog post and would like to receive similar content in the future, please consider following my profile. By following my profile @gaddamarunkumar , you will receive updates on new blog posts and other content related to ABAP development best practices.

Follow the SAP BTP ABAP environment Topic page: SAP BTP

This page is a great resource for staying up-to-date on the latest news and developments in the SAP ABAP environment. You can follow the page to receive updates, post and answer questions, and read other posts on the topic

entry.title: Class zdmo_cl_fe_travel_generator can't generate Package
entry.content:
Hi Community Experts, 

I having a problem with class zdmo_cl_fe_travel_generator when run the program suppose to generate a Package to add to the library but when i run the class the console is empty and i figure out why. One different that i found was my sub account on SAP BTP is US East but my friend who have a sub account Singapore can run the class zdmo_cl_fe_travel_generator normally and generated a Package to use.

Can someone help me generate the package from class zdmo_cl_fe_travel_generator i really want to learn more about SAP BTP.

Note: I also try running the class zdmo_cl_fe_travel_generator on my other friend eclipse which sub account is also US East but didn't work only the sub account Singapore can generate the package.

Thanks & Regards,

entry.title: Pagination / Lazy Loading / "Load more" not working in Fiori Elements Table Facet with Custom Entity
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I built a Fiori Elements App with an Object Page. On the Object Page, I created a Facet resulting in a Responsive Table:

 @ui: { facet: [  { id: 'History',
                    purpose: #STANDARD,
                    type: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE,
                    label: 'History',
                    targetElement: '_Log',
                    position: 40 } ],
         identification: [ { position: 1 } ],
         lineItem: [ { position: 1, label: 'Internal ID' } ] }

The referenced association "_Log" is refering to a Custom Entity that implements pagination as follows:

DATA(top) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_page_size( ).

    IF top < 0.
      top = 50.
    ENDIF.


    DATA(skip) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).

    IF top IS NOT INITIAL OR skip IS NOT INITIAL.
      /iwbep/cl_mgw_data_util=>paging( EXPORTING is_paging = VALUE #( top  = top
                                                                      skip = skip )
                                       CHANGING  ct_data   = lt_result ).
    ENDIF.

    IF io_request->is_total_numb_of_rec_requested( ).
      io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( lt_result ) ).
    ENDIF.
    io_response->set_data( lt_result ).

The problem is that the responsive table does not show the "Load more" button and is not scrollable:

Hence there is no possibility to trigger a second page call (normally done via "load more" or scrolling) and the amount of line items is always limited to the initial amount (in my case 10). All further entries are cut off.

Do you guys see a mistake in the implementation?
Thanks in advance for your help! 😊

Best regards,
Lukas

entry.title: Re: REDUCE a decilmal value
entry.content:
Thanks @marcelom_bovo for the answer.

it working fine.

entry.title: Re: Drill-Down in SAP ByD Excel Add-in
entry.content: Thanks for your answer. But we are not talking about excel itself, but about SAP Business ByDesign excel add-in. As an example you insert Financial Statement report and by clicking on any number in hierarchy you get to the data which is the source of initial aggregation. Something similar to BW SAP BEx Analyzer if you have used it.

entry.title: How to automatically start the SAP Management Console (SAP Service)
entry.content:
how to automatically start the SAP Management Console (SAP Service)

This article explains how to automatically start the SAP Management Console (SAP Service) when booting up the SAP cloud or physical system. Generally, to ensure that any program starts automatically during the boot process, we need to add that application to the scheduled tasks and assign a specific time for it to launch. However, while the SAP Management application may open, the SAP necessary services will not start automatically and will need to be initiated manually.

 After installing the SAP application, we need to check two settings in the Windows Services.

 Step 1:

 If the startup type is (Automatic Delayed Start) in Windows Services, the option will only start the application but will not initiate the SAP service. To run the SAP service, we must log into the system and start the service manually using the service username and password.

 

Step 2

 If the service associated with the account name and instance name does not start at startup, we need to log into the system and manually start the SAP MMC service.

 Solution for this issue:  We should select two options: one is Type: Automatic on General Tab, and the other is to log on with the.\SAP service.

entry.title: How to add email recipients to EWA
entry.content:

kindly help me to enable the delivery of EWA reports to multiple email ids.

entry.title: Re: How to add email recipients to EWA
entry.content:
Hi,

You can find detailed information on how to configure and add email recipients to EWA in KBA 2282944 - "EarlyWatch Alert: Solution Manager 7.2 How to Setup / Configure EWA Reports or Add Email Recipients".

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Pagination / Lazy Loading / "Load more" not working i...
entry.content:
Can you check if lines( lt_result ) really to total number of available lines is.

My guess is that it is a smaller amount, probably only 50?

So something like:

  SELECT * FROM zlog INTO TABLE lt_result WHERE ... .
  DATA(lv_total) = lines( lt_result ). 
 io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lv_total ).

or like us the lines before paging:

DATA(lv_total_count) = lines( lt_result ).

IF top IS NOT INITIAL OR skip IS NOT INITIAL.
  /iwbep/cl_mgw_data_util=>paging( EXPORTING is_paging = VALUE #( top  = top
                                                                  skip = skip )
                                   CHANGING  ct_data   = lt_result ).
ENDIF.

" Set total count based on original data, not paginated result
IF io_request->is_total_numb_of_rec_requested( ).
  io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lv_total_count ).
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Idoc for Material Change
entry.content: It is good for nothing. Copy unstructed information is not helpfull.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Account Determination per company code for...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

You're absolutely right to question this your understanding aligns with how Automatic Account Determination works in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (and in ECC or private cloud as well).

Automatic Account Determination for Foreign Currency Valuation is not configured at the company code level.
Instead, it's generally configured at the chart of accounts (CoA) level or valuation area level, which typically corresponds to the CoA used by one or more company codes.

Here’s a few details on how it works :

In the Manage Automatic Account Determination app (or configuration step), the system uses:

  • Transaction Key (e.g., KDF, KDB, etc. for exchange rate differences)
  • Chart of Accounts
  • And sometimes valuation modifiers (like valuation area or valuation method, depending on scope)

So, when you define which G/L accounts should be posted to for foreign currency valuation differences, you define them per CoA, not per company code.

 

I hope this will help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Key Tables
entry.content:
Dear All,

Please let me know where I can find ALL the Tables for FI, CO, MM, SD, PP, & QM? I have access to the Partner portal.

entry.title: Extension fields for workforce replication into S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear Team,

Currently integrating Workforce master data into S/4HANA Public Cloud using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) and SOAP APIs, and we need to use custom fields in our process. 

Therefore we have two queries: 

  1. Inbound (CPI → S/4): It seems custom fields are not available in this setup via SOAP APIs. Is there a way to enable or work around this?
  2. Outbound (S/4 → CPI): When you add custom fields to Workforce, events can’t be triggered or don’t include the custom fields in the payload. How can you send these fields to CPI — via events, CDS views, or another method?

Any guidance on how to implement custom fields in this scenario would be appreciated!

Best Regards. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Extension fields for workforce replication into S/4HA...
entry.content:
Hello, 

Custom fields are currently not available when integrating Workforce data via CPI and SOAP APIs in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This functionality is only available when using Master Data Integration (MDI). More information is available here: Extension Fields for Workers | SAP Help Portal.

 

However, starting from CE2508, support for custom fields will be added for Local Workers created through the Manage Workforce app. 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Position-Specific Competencies in Job Profile Builder...
entry.content:
@namsheedks1  - To achieve position-based competencies in SAP SuccessFactors modules like Performance Management, Succession Planning, and Career Development Planning (CDP), you’ll need to go beyond standard configurations. Some ways to do this is as below: 

  • Performance Management (PM Forms) By default: Only Job Role-linked competencies are pulled into PM forms. 
    To include Position-Specific Competencies: Use custom business rules or Integration Center to dynamically fetch competencies from the Position object. Modify the Performance Form Template to include a dynamic section that reads competencies from the Position via MDF (Metadata Framework). Alternatively, use Role-Based Permissions (RBP) and custom fields to display position-specific competencies manually.
  • Succession Planning By default: Succession views (like Job Role Details) show competencies tied to the Job Role. 
  • To enable Position-Based Succession Planning: Use Position Management to define competencies at the Position level. Enable Position-based nominations in Succession Planning. This allows you to: Nominate successors to a specific position (not just a role). Define required competencies for that position. Match candidates based on those competencies 1. This approach is especially useful for critical roles or regional variations.
  • Career Development Planning (CDP) By default: Career Worksheet uses competencies from the Job Role. To include Position-Specific Competencies: Use custom MDF objects or custom logic to pull competencies from the Position object. Alternatively, create custom Career Paths that reflect position-specific requirements. Use Talent Intelligence Hub (if enabled) to map skills more flexibly, though full Position-level integration is still evolving.

How to Include Position-Based Competencies

  1. PM Forms - Job Role only - Custom MDF logic, business rules, or template changes
  2. Succession - Job Role only - Enable Position-based nominations and define competencies at Position level
  3. CDP - Job Role only - Custom logic or use of Talent Intelligence Hub

SAP SuccessFactors Performance & Goals

SAP SuccessFactors Succession & Development

entry.title: Re: Key Tables
entry.content: Go to SE16N - table DD02L: it contains all the tables in the system. Use field "Application class" (APPLCLASS) to filter out tables per module.

entry.title: Gender Dimension problem
entry.content:
Hi @YSE-1 

I'm trying to understand what I'm doing wrong with Gender Dimension. 

I configured it as it is in the Manual Implementation guide, yet no matter what Measure I chose, everytime I analyze by Gender Dimension, I get almost all of the records as unallocated (from 17k employees, 2 female, 2 male and the rest unallocated) 

But this is impossible for 2 reasons: Gender is mandatory in our SF instance from where we take the data into WFA and when I chose Drill to Detail and select those unallocated employees and search them up in the system, they all have gender attached.

 

When I  click for validation or when I'm running the initial fact load, there are no errors, everything is successful. I've put some screenshots for more context.

What do you think is the cause for this behaviour? Thank you for your help! 

SAP SuccessFactors Workforce Analytics

entry.title: Re: Filter items in a section based on a selected or chec...
entry.content:
I don't know if this is possible in Elements, but in Freestyle I achieved something similiar by:

  1. when item pressed event gets fired
  2. the eventHandlerFunction gets the values of the pressed item
  3. call your backend with the parameters (e.g. SQL: WHERE column > itemValue)
  4. set the returned data into the underlying model of the table

Hope this helps a little bit!

Please let me know if there’s anything else you recommend checking.

Best regards,
Sanjay

Place

deletion flag

billing block

entry.title: Purchase document is allocated to asset
entry.content:
Whenever I am trying to make an advance payment into ''Public Cloub'' against asset purchase order with special gl indicator A, system is not allowing to do so giving the below mentioned error. Please solve this issue

Purchase document is allocated to asset

Diagnosis

The asset number   assigned in the purchase document


o is different from the asset number entered manually

o cannot be transferred, because the 'Asset number' field was hidden
for the account in question

System Response

In the first instance a warning appears, drawing your attention to the
different account assignments. In the second instance an error message
appears, because the asset number must be transferred from the purchase
order but the necessary field is hidden.

Procedure

If you receive the warning, check which asset number is the correct
number for this down payment.

If you receive an error message, you must either choose another special
G/L indicator for down payments so that it is possible to assign an
asset number to the relevant general ledger account, or you must change.

entry.title: Re: Getting error in Eclipse ADT "Business Catalog is not...

Checked the new authorizations from the above Note, they are already assigned to my user id. One more thing, from Fiori tile - Maintain Application Job template, I copied standard template and maintained my custom template as step2 along with the standard template as step1. I am trying to hide step1 while creating Application jobs from Fiori tile, I am still getting step1 selection screen even when I hide it. Any suggestion on this to hide standard template as we cannot delete it?

entry.title: Business partner in Human resources, field override
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are synching our employees into business partners now in an S4 Hana on prem.

I have noticed that the user department in SU01 is synched from the long text of the org. unit, and we would like change it for the SHORT text of the org unit.

I have been checking enhancement spot /SHCM/B_EE_BP_SYNC, and I can see a lot fields in there, but I can't find the department.

When the user is synched, I can see the department is taken from field WORKPLACE from ADDRESS_DATA_CORE, but I cannot find it in the Badi/Spot.

Any guiding light to check if this can be done wihout an implicit enhancement?

Thanks,

Pablo.

entry.title: Re: How can I add a new SAP Managed Tag for blog posts and edit existing tags?
entry.content: @papri_rudra29 You can find the list of all SAP Managed tags here: https://community.sap.com/t5/all-sap-managed-tags/ct-p/managed-tags

entry.title: Re: How to get notified by email when Application Autosca...
entry.content:
Hi, Thanks a lot for the helpful suggestion! Following your guidance, I was able to successfully receive notifications when my app scaled down. Here's what I did step by step:
1) I updated the Alert Notification service instance using: cf update-service SERVICE_NAME -c "{\"enableCloudControllerAuditEvents\": true}"
2) In the Alert Notification cockpit, I created a subscription with the following conditions: eventType is equal to audit.app.process.scale resource.resourceName is equal to APP_NAME
3) I then triggered the event manually using: cf scale APP_NAME -i 1
After this setup, I was able to receive the email notification successfully. Thanks again for pointing me in the right direction.

entry.title: Implementação da NT 2024.003
entry.content:
Olá, pessoal!

Estou utilizando o SAP S/4HANA 2022 e apliquei a nota técnica 3527792_E_20250516 com sucesso. Também realizei todas as parametrizações manuais indicadas na documentação da nota.

Porém, ao acessar a transação J1B2N, os novos campos que deveriam ser disponibilizados conforme a nota não estão sendo exibidos.

Já validei:

  • A aplicação da nota no sistema via SNOTE;

  • As configurações manuais (views e SPRO);

  • Permissões de usuário;

  • E cache da transação.

Gostaria de saber:

  1. Existe alguma BAdI (por exemplo, J_1BNF_ADD_DATA) que precise ser implementada ou ajustada para que os campos apareçam?

  2. Alguém enfrentou o mesmo problema após aplicar essa nota?

  3. Existe alguma outra nota SAP complementar que deva ser aplicada?

Agradeço desde já qualquer orientação!

entry.title: SAP AI Launchpad Generic Secret Creation
entry.content:
Hi, 

I'm exploring SAP AI Launchpad Standard and I have issue while creating Generic Secret.

I'm using SAP BTP Object Store to store my data. During creation of Generic Secret I've received following error: 

{
    "error": {
        "code": "400",
        "message": "'authentication' is missing in data."
    }
}
I added two labels: 
document-grounding : true
documentRepositoryType : S3

With those labels (document-grounding to be specific) i got an error 

, my secret structure looks as follows: 

{"access_key_id": "base64encoded",
"bucket": "base64encoded",
"host": "base64encoded", 
"region": "base64encoded", 
"secret_access_key": "base64encoded", 
"uri":"base64encoded",
"username":"base64encoded"}

so indeed there is no authentication field but what should passed there in S3 scenario?
Additionaly, if I try to create generic secret without label document-grounding:true it is possible but if I then go to Grounding Management and try to create Data Repository following error appears

Error Code: 404
Message: Received not found from document grounding api = {'requestId': '1394b8d8-88a1-46ee-b840-3b6408aef4dd', 'code': 404, 'message': 'Destination in Configuration not found'}

BTW. I did try to create Generic Secret via Postman as well and the same error appears.
Any ideas? 
Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Payload Examples for API Journal Entry – Post
entry.content:
Hi  @Wesley, thanks for these great examples and we are now able to post into our S/4 HANA 2023 FPS03 system via API Management. But we do have one business need. We need to have external number ranges, as the master system is an external system and we like to have same document number in S/4 and other system. Is ther any chance to use external number ranges with this SAOP Webservice?

Regards Tobias

entry.title: Can SAP BTP Alert Notification Service send SMS directly?
entry.content: I’m using SAP BTP Alert Notification Service for monitoring and alerting. I want to send SMS messages directly (without external API providers or hardware). Does SAP Alert Notification support SMS as a native channel? If not, what are the best practices to send SMS via webhook integration?SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTP

entry.title: Re: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: Modeling Update (Join)
entry.content:
Hello @Paulo_Moncada,
Thanks for the information about the changes. But IMO, this philosophy of creating a "worldwide beta-testing" is at minimum worrying. It is already bad enough when we have unannounced updates, even if they are for quality-of-life changes. But this update makes no sense. We are forced to work differently only for the join function, while model joins, views, etc have the prior behavior. Worse than that, the change was pushed without a way to simply continue to operate on the prior behavior in case of:
1- difficulties of adaptation midweek - impacting project development
2- issues with the rollout, like the missing field auto completion.

entry.title: Successfactors LMS : Overdue learning notification
entry.content: A user, has been receiving email notifications from the LMS that state he needs to complete a course. However, he has already completed the course, as is reflected in users learning history. User no longer has the course in  assignments, so I’m not sure what would cause to be picked up in the notification email.

entry.title: Re: Learn BTP, Joule, and AI – as SAP Business Analyst or...
entry.content:
Hi @KavneetKaur 
Please access our Artificial Intelligence learn by product site where you can unlock the power of AI to streamline operations, boost productivity, and drive smarter, data-informed decisions—faster than ever.
Please explore our search function to find the learning that suits your inquiry, access the search results  for SAP Joule and SAP AI Core here 
Browse our Data and Analytics site and learn how to get the most value from data with tools that help you deliver insights and transform data planning and analysis.
Explore our SAP Build Process Automation site and learn how to build and automate workflows with bots and AI using SAP Build Process Automation. 
Access our SAP Integration Suite site and learn how to connect systems, automate business processes, and harmonize experiences across the extended enterprise.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Performance Management Story Report
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created the  story report and it is working fine for me even if I am proxying anyone else, but if the same report is run by other users Performance Rating appears in decimal and column names also appearing differently in some cases.

And in few cases output is blank but once I proxied, I got the output.

Kindly suggest.

Regards,
P

entry.title: AI Launchpad, Generic Secret Creation Error, Creation of Data Repository for Grounding not possible
entry.content:
Hi, 

I'm exploring SAP AI Launchpad Standard and I have issue while creating Generic Secret.

I'm using SAP BTP Object Store to store my data. During creation of Generic Secret I've received following error: 

{
    "error": {
        "code": "400",
        "message": "'authentication' is missing in data."
    }
}
I added two labels: 
document-grounding : true
documentRepositoryType : S3

With those labels (document-grounding to be specific) i got an error 

, my secret structure looks as follows: 

 

{"access_key_id": "base64encoded",
"bucket": "base64encoded",
"host": "base64encoded", 
"region": "base64encoded", 
"secret_access_key": "base64encoded", 
"uri":"base64encoded",
"username":"base64encoded"}

 

so indeed there is no authentication field but what should passed there in S3 scenario?
Additionaly, if I try to create generic secret without label document-grounding:true it is possible but if I then go to Grounding Management and try to create Data Repository following error appears

Error Code: 404
Message: Received not found from document grounding api = {'requestId': '1394b8d8-88a1-46ee-b840-3b6408aef4dd', 'code': 404, 'message': 'Destination in Configuration not found'}

BTW. I did try to create Generic Secret via Postman as well and the same error appears.
Any ideas? 
Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Batch-specific / -neutral available quantity in storage type when picking for supply to producit
entry.content: Hello castin, I had this button in mind.
